transcript:
Watch that orange orb — it looks like it's lighting up each word as it appears, but it's not. It's always trailing behind, chasing something that already happened.

That gap is literally coded in. There's a variable called fundamental lag, set to three — meaning the eye only ever examines the word from three steps back, never the one currently forming. You can see it in the animate function: generation index counts up as new words spawn, but examination index is deliberately calculated as generation index minus that lag. The eye is mathematically forbidden from catching up.

And the words don't just sit there once written — they sink. Downward, into a spiral, at what's literally named "descent rate" in the code. The artist calls it "the sediment of self." So the further back a thought falls, the deeper it drops, like memory settling into archive.

What gets me is that the piece never resolves the gap — it just keeps orbiting the same fixed distance behind itself, forever describing a presence it can't reach.
